frameworks.name: representational similarity analysis
frameworks.one_line: geometry of multi-task neural representations measured across cortical regions

frameworks.name: multi-layer neural network model
frameworks.one_line: trained to transform empirical visual to motor representations

core_question: How are multi-task cognitive representations organized geometrically and topographically across human cortex?
key_assumptions: task representations can be compared via representational similarity
alignment between regions reflects shared computational structure
cortical hierarchy provides an organizing axis for cognition
manipulations: 26 cognitive tasks performed by the same subjects during fMRI
neural network training regime (transformation-optimized vs generic signal propagation)
key_findings: cortical topography of representational alignment follows a sensory-association-motor gradient
multi-task dimensionality shows compression-then-expansion along this gradient
compression-then-expansion emerges in models only when optimized for sensory-to-motor transformation
interpretations: hierarchically structured cortical representations reflect optimization for sensory-to-motor transformation
generic signal propagation is insufficient to produce the empirical geometry
